About: It is about an English and Welsh dynasty that held the throne of England from 1485 to 1603.
Affects: It affects the future of the Crown.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Tudors of Penmynydd.
It is associated with Catherine of Valois.
It is associated with the House of Lancaster.
It is associated with the House of Plantagenet.
It is associated with the Scottish House of Stuart.
It is associated with the House of Beaufort.
It is associated with the Wars of the Roses.
It is associated with the House of York.
It is associated with the Principality of Wales.
It is associated with the Kingdom of Ireland.
BelongsTo: It belongs to English and Welsh nobility.
ConsistsOf: It consists of five monarchs: Henry VII, Henry VIII, Edward VI, Mary I and Elizabeth I.
CreatedAround: It was created around the time of the Wars of the Roses.
FoundedBy: It was founded by Henry VII.
HasA: It has a period of 118 years of rule.
It has a heraldic emblem of the Tudor rose.
It has a connection to the present 21st century.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by issues around royal succession.
It is characterized by the English Reformation in religion.
Influenced: It influenced the future of the Crown.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Wars of the Roses.
IsA: It is an English and Welsh dynasty.
It is a cadet house of the Plantagenets.
KnownAs: It is known as the Tudor period.
It is known as the Elizabethan Era.
KnownFor: It is known for holding the throne of England from 1485 to 1603.
It is known for its five monarchs.
It is known for uniting the former warring factions of Lancaster and York.
It is known for achieving the full union of England and the Principality of Wales.
It is known for asserting English authority over the Kingdom of Ireland.
It is known for maintaining the nominal English claim to the Kingdom of France.
NamedAfter: It is named after the Tudors of Penmynydd.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Wars of the Roses.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the Lordship of Ireland.
It occurred in the Kingdom of Ireland.
PartOf: It is part of English history.
It is part of Welsh history.
